title: centrosema insulanum
intro: Centrosema insulanum, also known as the bush copra or island centrosema, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to tropical regions and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds, as well as showing potential in treating conditions such as diabetes and cancer. Centrosema insulanum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
